name: HYPERBOLIC GRAVITY CHAMBER — TEMPO PROTOCOL
fantasy: Step into the chamber where every rep fights the weight of worlds. Move slow, hold the tension, breathe through it — control is the whole game.
tagline: Control the tension. Breathe through it.
core_rule: Tension is the challenge — breath is the release.
core_rule_enforcement: Breathe continuously on every rep and hold — NEVER hold your breath or bear down (Valsalva), which spikes blood pressure dangerously. Holds run to a TARGET time, never to failure or 'unlimited'. Load stays light — the difficulty is tempo, not weight. Reckless grinding is not rewarded.
mechanic: TEMPO + VOICE-COUNT CADENCE. Bodyweight (or light-dumbbell) reps performed at a controlled tempo — slow eccentric, brief hold, controlled concentric — plus isometric holds. A voice coach counts the cadence ('down 1...2...3... hold... up 1...2...3... hold'). Each stage is themed as a rising 'gravity multiplier' (motivational only — see sourcing_note). Stages change the VOICE-COUNT MODE and the TEMPO, not the load.
sourcing_note: Grounded in the deep-research report (protocol-src/research/gravity-research-report.md). HONESTY NOTE from the research: 2025-2026 meta-analyses show slow tempo does NOT build more muscle/strength than normal tempo — 'slower = more gains' is NOT supported. So this campaign is framed as a SAFE, CONTROLLED tension / strength-endurance / focus method (eccentric work IS a real strength stimulus), NOT as superior muscle growth. The 'gravity multiplier' and slow-tempo theming are motivational packaging, flagged as fantasy, never a real training variable. Refuted (not claimed): that eccentric is superior to concentric. Safety findings (breathe-don't-Valsalva BP spikes, novel-eccentric DOMS/rhabdo risk even at low intensity, repeated-bout ramping, target hold times, light load, reps under ~10s) are peer-reviewed and encoded as hard rules below.

hard.label: MAX GRAVITY
hard.note: Total time per rep stays UNDER ~10s — research shows reps over 10s/rep are actually inferior. Never exceed it.

hard_never_requires: Holding the breath or bearing down (Valsalva)

voice_count_modes.none: No count — the athlete finds their own reps (used to set a baseline).
voice_count_modes.reps: Coach counts completed reps out loud ('one... two... three...').
voice_count_modes.cadence: Coach paces the tempo on the way down AND up with the hold: 'down 1...2...3... hold... up 1...2...3... hold'. This is the signature mode.

safety_scaffolding.why: Slow-tempo + isometric bodyweight work is a real trainable method, but novel eccentric/high-TUT loading reliably causes DOMS, can trigger rhabdomyolysis even after LOW-intensity exercise, and breath-holding during holds/grinding reps spikes blood pressure severely.
rules: BREATHE, NEVER VALSALVA: continuous breathing on every rep and hold — exhale on exertion, never bear down. The coach cues breathing; a persistent breath-hold flag stops the set.
TARGET HOLDS, NOT FAILURE: every isometric hold runs to a predetermined target time, then stop — never 'unlimited' or to failure.
GRADUAL RAMP (repeated-bout effect): gravity stages increase tempo/hold difficulty gradually; Stage 9 is a deliberate back-off so novel eccentric volume never spikes. Do not skip ahead.
LIGHT LOAD ONLY: bodyweight or light dumbbells; heavy load is never prescribed. Difficulty comes from tempo, not weight.
REPS UNDER ~10s: total time per rep capped under 10 seconds (past that is inferior and riskier).
user_facing_stop_criteria: Dizziness, lightheadedness, or feeling faint (especially on standing after holds)
Headache or vision changes during a hold (blood-pressure warning)
Severe or unusual muscle pain, swelling, or dark cola-colored urine (rhabdomyolysis signs)
Chest pain or shortness of breath
safety_scaffolding.stop_action: Any stop criterion halts the session immediately with a plain-language caution (breathe/rest; dark urine or severe swelling = seek medical advice), and applies no XP penalty.
safety_scaffolding.screening_note: People with high blood pressure or cardiovascular conditions should keep holds short, load light, and breathe freely — surface this on first entry.

equipment_profiles.rule: Fully bodyweight. Light dumbbells are optional and never required; a wall (for wall-sits) is environmental. NO heavy load.

achievements.name: Baseline Set
achievements.trigger: Complete Stage 1 (find your reps, no count)

achievements.name: In the Cadence
achievements.trigger: Complete Stage 3 with the voice cadence

achievements.name: Never Break Breath
achievements.trigger: Complete a full stage with zero breath-hold flags

achievements.name: Iron Hold
achievements.trigger: Hit every isometric hold target in a stage

achievements.name: Adaptation
achievements.trigger: Complete the Stage 9 back-off / recovery day

response: Reduce sets/reps or shorten hold targets
Never push a broken tempo — quality over count

safety_stop: Headache or vision change during a hold

final_stage.name: The Chamber's Limit — 1000x Gravity
final_stage.rule: A target-based tempo gauntlet through the campaign's movements — hit the targets with clean tempo and steady breathing. NEVER to failure. Enter rested (Stage 9 was the back-off).
final_stage.outcome: FINAL CLEAR → victory screen + 1000x Gravity / Chamber Master achievements.
